**sdd/governance/proposal**

## Proposal: governance — v2 (floor + audit + golden path)

**Intent:** Enforce brain's four load-bearing invariants as far as the environment allows and make compliance the path of least resistance. Core principle: enforce observable OUTPUTS wherever a gate can fail-closed; guide irreducible JUDGMENT with in-context docs. Never claim to enforce what cannot be mechanically verified.

**4 invariants:** (1) no merge without approved ticket, (2) no PR >400 changed lines without `size:exception`, (3) memory dumped before close, (4) ADR for every decision.

**Honest boundary:** a fully tool-independent HARD guarantee does not exist on SaaS — the chokepoint at merge is platform-owned. What IS universal: a default-on local floor + post-hoc audit.

**Architecture — three composed layers (v2 model):**
- Floor (always-on, tool-independent): generic checks library (node/git) + client-hook suite (commit-msg/pre-commit/pre-push) + brain:audit (re-verify merged history). The universal backbone.
- Hard gate (additive, capability-aware): VCS adapter — protectBranch() returns {enforced, reason, remedy}, never crashes; capabilities() probed not hardcoded. Platform enforcement where the tier allows.
- Golden path: self-gating brain verbs (brain:start/check/save/ship/next) unifying human + agent.

**5 slices:**
- S1 Foundation ✅ Done — ADR-0014, PR template, governance.ignoreList, managed-paths
- S2 Platform CI ✅ Done — governance.yml issue-link + diff-size (one additive adapter, not the universal guarantee)
- S3 Capability-aware adapter — protectBranch → {enforced,reason,remedy}, capabilities(), brain:governance status
- S4 The floor — generic checks library (diff-size, issue-link, adr-presence, memory-presence) + client-hook suite (commit-msg/pre-commit/pre-push) + brain:audit (re-verify merged history, the universal teeth)
- S5 Golden path — brain:start/check/save/ship/next verbs + --no-verify policy (repo:check prohibited-ref + harness PreToolUse hook)
- Phase 3 deferred: GitLab/Bitbucket protectBranch + pre-receive, rulesets, full session_summary check

**Non-goals:** full Inv-3 session_summary check (engram JSONL schema unconfirmed + no session/{issue} convention — Phase 3); enforcing capture quality; enforcing "sliced well"; GitLab protectBranch; GitHub rulesets; .github/** as managed glob.

**Where:** openspec/changes/governance/proposal.md (this topic — v2 reshape). Files: all prior + scripts/governance/checks/{diff-size,issue-link,adr-presence,memory-presence}.mjs + scripts/hooks/commit-msg + scripts/hooks/pre-commit + scripts/brain-audit.mjs + scripts/brain-{start,check,save,ship,next}.mjs + harness PreToolUse config.

content:
**sdd/governance/design**

## Design: governance — v2 (floor + additive hard gate + golden path)

**Canonical design doc:** docs/inbox/workflow-governance-layer.md

**Architecture:** generic checks library → composed at three points:
1. Floor (always-on): client hooks (commit-msg/pre-commit/pre-push via core.hooksPath) + brain:audit (re-verify merged history). The floor does NOT fall back to the adapter; they compose independently.
2. Hard gate (additive): VCS adapter — protectBranch() returns {enforced:true} or {enforced:false, reason, remedy}; capabilities() probes the API (never hardcodes matrix); brain:governance status reports per-consumer.
3. Golden path: brain:start/check/save/ship/next self-gating verbs.

**Slices:**
- S1 ✅ Done: ADR-0014, PR template, governance.ignoreList migration, two managed paths
- S2 ✅ Done: governance.yml issue-link + diff-size (one additive adapter, CI-conditional)
- S3: protectBranch → {enforced,reason,remedy}; capabilities() via API probe; brain:governance status; GOVERNANCE_JOBS stays ['issue-link','diff-size'] (NO new CI jobs in S4)
- S4: Generic checks library (scripts/governance/checks/ — diff-size.mjs, issue-link.mjs, adr-presence.mjs, memory-presence.mjs); hook suite (new commit-msg + pre-commit; extend pre-push); brain:audit (scripts/brain-audit.mjs — git log --merges + 4 checks per merge + FAIL attribution)
- S5: brain:start/check/save/ship/next scripts; --no-verify prohibited in check-config + harness PreToolUse hook

**Key design decisions:**
- GOVERNANCE_JOBS stays ['issue-link','diff-size'] — S4 does NOT add memory-gate or decision-gate as CI jobs (v1 pattern). Checks move to the floor (hooks + audit) not more CI jobs.
- protectBranch() never throws/crashes — returns {enforced,reason,remedy} shape always.
- capabilities() PROBED not hardcoded — the tier matrix rots; probe + cache the 403/result.
- brain:audit verifies OUTCOME (merged history), forge-proof, attributes violations to SHA.
- The --no-verify policy: prohibited-ref in repo:check (tool-independent) + harness hook (agent-layer) + audit catches what slips through.

**File changes (new vs v1):** Removed: memory-gate and decision-gate YAML jobs from governance.yml. Added: scripts/governance/checks/*.mjs, scripts/hooks/commit-msg, scripts/hooks/pre-commit, scripts/brain-audit.mjs, scripts/brain-{start,check,save,ship,next}.mjs, scripts/brain-governance-status.mjs, harness PreToolUse config.

**Where:** openspec/changes/governance/design.md

content:
**sdd/governance/spec**

## Spec: governance — v2 requirements

**Canonical design:** docs/inbox/workflow-governance-layer.md

**Requirement counts by slice:**
- S1: REQ-S1-1 through REQ-S1-5 (5 reqs) ✅ Done
- S2: REQ-S2-1 through REQ-S2-4 (4 reqs) ✅ Done (reframed: S2 is ONE additive adapter)
- S3: REQ-S3-1 through REQ-S3-8 (8 reqs) — added REQ-S3-7 (capabilities()) and REQ-S3-8 (brain:governance status); updated REQ-S3-1 (protectBranch returns {enforced,reason,remedy})
- S4: REQ-S4-1 through REQ-S4-6 (6 reqs) — REPLACED v1-S4 (memory-gate/decision-gate YAML) with: REQ-S4-1 generic checks library (4 pure functions), REQ-S4-2/3/4 hook suite (commit-msg/pre-commit/pre-push), REQ-S4-5/6 brain:audit
- S5: REQ-S5-1 through REQ-S5-6 (6 reqs) — NEW: brain:start/check/save/ship/next verbs + --no-verify policy
- Epic: REQ-E-1/E-2 (bootstrap order, branch coordination)

**Key spec changes from v1:**
- REQ-S3-1 updated: protectBranch() MUST return {enforced:true} or {enforced:false, reason, remedy}; MUST NOT throw. (v1 had returns {protected:true/false})
- REQ-S3-7 NEW: capabilities() MUST probe (not hardcode), return {hardEnforcement: 'available'|'unavailable'|'unknown', detail}
- REQ-S3-8 NEW: brain:governance status reports all 3 layers per-consumer
- REQ-S4-1 through REQ-S4-6 REPLACED v1's REQ-S4-1 (memory-gate CI) + REQ-S4-2 (adr-gate CI) + REQ-S4-3 (arch-surface heuristic)
- REQ-S5-1 through REQ-S5-6 are entirely new
- Epic non-goal updated: system MUST NOT treat client hooks as a substitute for the platform hard gate

**Testability annotations:** All S4 check functions are unit-testable (pure, no CI needed). brain:audit is unit-testable with git fixture. All S5 verbs have injectable seams for VCS adapter.

**Gaps:** G3 = Invariant-3 honest residual (memory-presence proves .memory/ changed, NOT session_summary quality — Phase 3). G6 = brain:next state model (implementation decision). G7 = harness PreToolUse format (harness-specific implementation).

**Where:** openspec/changes/governance/specs/governance/spec.md

**mrCreate adapter verb** (added as S5.4 dependency):

- [x] gitlab.mrCreate() stub added to scripts/vcs/providers/gitlab.mjs — returns {url:null, error:'Phase 3'}
- [x] 3 tests added to providers.test.mjs

## Key Design Decisions (S5)

### S5.1 — Branch creation is local git (not VCS API)
Branch creation uses `git checkout -b` directly (local git op, always works). The VCS adapter is only used for issue metadata (issueView). This is more honest than going through the API for a local branch.

### S5.2 — brain:check uses dependency injection
All 4 checks + npmTest + repoCheck are passed as injectable fns. CLI wires real implementations. Tests use stubs. No process.exit in the exported function — only in the CLI guard.

### S5.5 — brain:next state machine priorities
1. no-branch (main/master/dev/develop/release/*) → brain:start
2. open-pr (PR exists for current headBranch) → monitor
3. no-memory (no uncommitted .memory/) → brain:save
4. checks-failing (repo:check exits non-zero) → brain:check
5. ready (everything ok) → brain:ship

Non-working branches: only main/master/dev/develop/release/* are treated as non-working. feature/* and gov/* are allowed as working branches (issue-number branches like feature/42-slug are working).

### S5.6 — no-verify-bypass rule placement
Rule added to brain/project/check-refs-rules.mjs (NOT brain/core/), keeping project-specific rules separate from core. onlyExt limits to .mjs/.js/.ts/.sh. Hook files (no extension) are naturally excluded by onlyExt. The rules file itself + the test file are in the rule's own exempt list.

### S5.7 — .claude/settings.json ships as managed path
The Claude Code harness hook travels with brain (like governance.yml). It's a NEW SESSION config — doesn't affect the current session. Already globally exempt from repo:check via BASE_GLOBAL_EXEMPT.

## Verification Results (S5)

- npm test: 353/353 pass (was 318 before S5; +35 new tests)
- npm run repo:check: green
- npm run brain:nav: green
- npm run brain:audit (live): runs without crash; --first-parent limits to 6 top-level integration merges (vs many more nested merges before fix); exits 1 (pre-governance historical violations — expected)
- Commit: 83f9a2e on gov/s5-golden-path (NOT pushed)

## Total Diff Size

**~1444 lines** (1442 insertions + 2 deletions). EXCEEDS the 400-line PR review budget by 3.6x.
Reason: 5 new verb pairs (impl + test) × ~150 lines each = ~750 + governance rules + audit fix + adapter + tests = 1444 total.

**Split recommendation if needed:**
- S5a: verbs (brain-start, brain-check, brain-save, brain-ship, brain-next) + mrCreate adapter — ~1250 lines

## Remaining Tasks

content:
**Reshaped governance openspec to v2 floor+audit+golden-path model**

**What:** Reshaped all 4 governance openspec artifacts (proposal.md, design.md, spec.md, tasks.md) from the v1 "GitHub branch protection" model to the v2 "floor + additive hard gate + golden path" model.

**Why:** The v2 model (docs/inbox/workflow-governance-layer.md) is tool-agnostic and honest about the boundary: no tool-independent HARD guarantee exists on SaaS. The floor (hooks + audit) is universal; the platform gate is additive and conditional.

**Key architectural differences v1→v2:**
- v1 S4: memory-gate + decision-gate as CI YAML jobs (platform-conditional). REMOVED.
- v2 S4: generic checks library (scripts/governance/checks/) + full hook suite (commit-msg/pre-commit/pre-push) + brain:audit (re-verify merged history). Tool-independent.
- v2 S5 (NEW): self-gating golden-path verbs (brain:start/check/save/ship/next) + --no-verify policy (prohibited in repo:check + harness PreToolUse hook)
- v2 S3: protectBranch() now returns {enforced, reason, remedy} (never crashes on 403); capabilities() PROBED not hardcoded; brain:governance status added.
- GOVERNANCE_JOBS stays ['issue-link','diff-size'] — S4 does NOT add new CI jobs.

**Where:** openspec/changes/governance/{proposal.md, design.md, specs/governance/spec.md, tasks.md}. Engram topics sdd/governance/{proposal,design,spec,tasks} all updated.

**Learned:** The floor and hard gate COMPOSE — they don't fall back on each other. This distinction is load-bearing: client hooks are bypassable (soft, universal) while the platform gate is hard but conditional. brain:audit is the forge-proof universal teeth — verifies the outcome over merged history regardless of how the merge happened.

content:
**Session summary: brain**

## Goal
Cerrar la capa de gobernanza, releasear, y dejar brain listo para que el usuario lo use en su proyecto MAÑANA (npm o pnpm).

## Accomplished
- **Epic gobernanza COMPLETO → main (PR #68, #67 closed)**: el modelo tool-agnóstico v2.
  - S1 foundation · S2 platform CI (adapter) · S3 capability-aware adapter (brain:protect/branchProtect/governance-status — reporta por-consumer qué soporta el tier) · S4 EL FLOOR (checks genéricos node/git + suite de hooks reliability-first + brain:audit = los dientes universales) · S5 golden path (brain:start/check/save/ship/next self-gating + política --no-verify).
  - ADR-0014. Design doc v2 en docs/inbox.
  - **Dogfooding probado en vivo**: commit-msg me bloqueó un commit sin #N; brain:audit cazó el chain; push normal (sin --no-verify) pasa el pre-push confiable.
  - **Incidente arreglado**: brain-protect corría al importarlo (faltaba CLI guard) → wrapped + test de regresión. (El repo es privado+free → branch protection da 403, no se activó nada.)
  - **Pre-push reliability fix**: el check de .memory pasó de hard-block a WARNING (re-materializa → churn → self-block → --no-verify). Es la precondición de la política.
- **Release v0.6.0** (PR #79) + **v0.6.1** (PR #88, pnpm fix). version-sync test: el README debe apuntar a la versión de package.json o npm test falla (enforce, no recordar).
- **pnpm install ARREGLADO Y VALIDADO**:
  - #86 (v0.6.1): removido el script `prepare` de package.json (pnpm 11 bloquea git-deps con build scripts → ERR_PNPM_GIT_DEP_PREPARE_NOT_ALLOWED). Era inútil para consumers (corre en clon temporal); env:init + day:start configuran hooksPath.
  - #89: removidos los pins `packageManager` de los fixtures (pnpm@9.0.0 etc. auto-switcheaban a versiones con bug de git-auth).
  - **fresh-install @ v0.6.1 con pnpm: PASA** (brain installed via pnpm, todo verde).
- **#80**: arreglados 3 bugs del fresh-install test de #72 (ORIGINAL_SRC antes de la modif; fixture sin git origin; .gitattributes path).
- **#82/#84/#83/#85**: day:start ya self-healea core.hooksPath (estaba); brain:upgrade ahora recuerda env:init; README v2 PM-agnóstico + nota de seguridad (env:init aditivo, no pisa nada; brain:upgrade solo managed paths) + comandos de gobernanza.

## Discoveries / decisiones clave
- **core.hooksPath es per-clon, NO committeado** (diseño de git). brain:upgrade trae los ARCHIVOS de hooks (managed); cada dev corre env:init una vez (o day:start self-healea). Por eso el AUDIT (no depende de config local) son los dientes universales.
- **L1 (branch protection) necesita GitHub Pro para repos privados, o público, o self-hosted** — ni branch protection ni rulesets en free+privado (403 verificado). El floor (hooks+audit) anda en todo repo/tier/plataforma; el hard gate es aditivo vía adapter.
- env:init NO pisa código ni historia: crea brain.config.json solo si falta (rellena campos vacíos, preserva valores), git config local, .env gitignored. brain:upgrade sobreescribe solo managed paths, nunca brain/project ni tu código.
- pnpm install de brain ANDA con pnpm actual (11). pnpm 9.0.0 tiene bug de git-auth (no usar).

## Next Steps (para el usuario, mañana)
- En su proyecto: auth a github → instalar v0.6.1 (npm o pnpm) → agregar aliases → brain:upgrade → env:init → day:start.
- Pendientes/refinamientos: activación brain:protect (decisión de tier public/Pro); audit scope Part-of/Closes (chained-PR semantics); commit-msg exenciones para merge/release; el branch feature/issue-11-cli-i18n abierto.

## Relevant Files (main, v0.6.1)
- scripts/governance/checks/*, scripts/brain-audit.mjs, scripts/brain-{start,check,save,ship,next,protect,governance-status}.mjs
- scripts/hooks/{commit-msg,pre-commit,pre-push}, .claude/settings.json (PreToolUse --no-verify block)
- scripts/readme-version.test.mjs (version-sync), test/fixtures/{npm,pnpm,yarn,bun}
- brain/project/decisions/adr-0014-workflow-governance.md, README.md, CHANGELOG.md
- package.json (sin prepare; 0.6.1)

content:
**sdd/issue-97-managed-paths/explore**

## Exploration: issue-97 — managed paths collision (scripts/ + .claude/settings.json)

### Current State

`brain/core/managed-paths.mjs` declares two colliding entries:
- `'scripts/**'` — brain claims the consumer's ROOT scripts/ directory and dumps ~87 files there on every upgrade
- `'.claude/settings.json'` — brain OVERWRITES the consumer's Claude Code permissions allowlist (plain copyFileSync in installer.mjs)

Synergy's `.claude/settings.json` is 4.7KB / 63 allow entries. Brain's `.claude/settings.json` is 15 lines / only the PreToolUse hook block. An upgrade would silently destroy the entire allowlist. Synergy's `.claude/settings.local.json` (another 200+ entries) is NOT in managed paths so it survives — but the consumer would lose the primary settings.json entirely.

Synergy currently has NO `scripts/` dir. If brain installed, 87 scripts would land there. If a consumer had `scripts/caso-uso-sesiones.sh`, it would survive (installer only copies, never deletes) but 87 more brain scripts would coexist — plus any shared filenames (e.g., `scripts/bootstrap.sh`) would be silently overwritten.

### Blast Radius — scripts/ → brain/scripts/ rename

**87 files to move** (git mv scripts/ brain/scripts/)

**Critical runtime references (break if not updated): ~74 across ~20 files**

Categories:
- **package.json script aliases**: 28 entries (`"node ./scripts/..."`, `"bash ./scripts/..."`, test glob)
- **bootstrap.sh**: 10 references incl. `git config core.hooksPath scripts/hooks` (THE key coupling)
- **install-tools.sh**: 1 reference
- **day-start.mjs**: 4 references incl. `const HOOKS_PATH = 'scripts/hooks'` (self-heal mechanism)
- **hooks (pre-push, pre-commit, post-merge)**: 6 `node "$repo_root/scripts/..."` calls
- **brain-save.mjs**: 1 (`scripts/memory/cli.mjs`)
- **brain-next.mjs**: 1 (`scripts/check-refs.mjs`)
- **memory/lib/auto-resume.mjs**: 1 (`scripts/memory/cli.mjs`)
- **brain/core/managed-paths.mjs**: 1 (the `'scripts/**'` entry itself)
- **verify-change.mjs**: 2 (scope matcher logic: `f.startsWith('scripts/')`)
- **check-refs.mjs**: 1 (exempt list)
- **brain/project/check-refs-rules.mjs**: 2 (exempt list)
- **test container scripts** (fresh-install + upgrade in-container.sh): 8 references
- **test/fixtures/*/package.json** (4 files): 8 references (2 per fixture)

**Test assertions that would fail: ~15 across 6 test files**
- installer.test.mjs: 5 hardcoded `scripts/` fixture paths
- check-refs.test.mjs: 3 `addTrackedFile(git, dir, 'scripts/...')` fixtures  
- diff-size.test.mjs + diff-size-count.test.mjs: 4 numstat fixture strings
- i18n/coverage.test.mjs: 2 (hardcoded `scripts/hooks` in translation assertion)
- managed-paths.test.mjs: 1 implicit (tests managed array content)

**Doc/comment references: ~85 (non-breaking but should update)**
- README.md: 7 references (layer diagram, install commands, table)
- openspec design/tasks/spec docs: ~50
- docs/inbox: 3
- Source file header comments: ~25

**Self-hosting issue**: Brain's own repo IS the `scripts/` dir. The rename requires a coordinated atomic commit (`git mv` + all reference updates together) to avoid broken intermediate state. You CANNOT do this incrementally while the scripts themselves are running.

**Consumer migration concern**: Any existing consumer with brain installed has brain scripts at root `scripts/`. After the rename, `brain:upgrade` would copy to `brain/scripts/` but leave orphaned files at `scripts/`. A CHANGELOG + cleanup step is needed.

**core.hooksPath transition gap**: `core.hooksPath` is per-clone (not committed). After upgrade to renamed version, `day:start` checks `existsSync(HOOKS_PATH/pre-push)` where HOOKS_PATH would now be `brain/scripts/hooks`. If the file doesn't exist yet (first run after upgrade, before files copied), it WARNS and skips self-heal — one-time "no hooks" window. Acceptable but should be documented.

### Namespace Decision

Options considered:
- `brain/scripts/` — RECOMMENDED: completes the brain/ namespace (core + project + scripts). Natural. No dot-dir awkwardness.
- `brain/core/scripts/` — Wrong: brain/core is for methodology/docs, not executable scripts
- `.brain/` — Unusual hidden-dir pattern, same blast radius
- Keep `scripts/` — Zero blast but doesn't fix the collision

`brain/scripts/` is the right answer. It matches the existing structure conceptually.

NX friction: minimal. NX's `namedInputs.default` is `{projectRoot}/**/*` (per-project, not workspace root), so brain scripts in `brain/scripts/` at NX workspace root don't interfere with NX build targets. Root `package.json` brain aliases (`brain:upgrade`, `day:start`, etc.) coexist fine with NX scripts.

### settings.json Merge Design

**Today**: `copyFileSync(src, dest)` — pure overwrite in `installer.mjs:copyManaged()`

**New design** (additive merge, similar to `mergeDefaults` already in installer.mjs):
```js
function mergeClaudeSettings(existingPath, brainSettings) {
  // Fresh consumer (no existing file) → write brain's as-is
  if (!existsSync(existingPath)) return brainSettings;
  
  const existing = JSON.parse(readFileSync(existingPath, 'utf8'));
  const result = { ...existing };
  
  // Merge hooks.PreToolUse additively (deduplicate by command string)
  if (brainSettings.hooks?.PreToolUse) {
    result.hooks = result.hooks ?? {};
    result.hooks.PreToolUse = result.hooks.PreToolUse ?? [];
    for (const brainEntry of brainSettings.hooks.PreToolUse) {
      const alreadyPresent = result.hooks.PreToolUse.some(
        e => JSON.stringify(e) === JSON.stringify(brainEntry)
      );
      if (!alreadyPresent) result.hooks.PreToolUse.push(brainEntry);
    }
  }
  // Preserve all other consumer keys (permissions, other hooks, etc.)
  return result;
}
```

Wiring: in `brain-upgrade.mjs`, instead of letting `.claude/settings.json` go through `copyManaged` plain copy, handle it specially (either remove from managed array and apply merge post-copy, or add a `smartMerge` list to the installer).

`settings.local.json` — already safe: not in managed paths, never touched.

Fresh consumer: works — no existing file → write brain's 15-line hook block as-is.
Existing consumer: `permissions.allow` and all other keys preserved; only `hooks.PreToolUse` additively extended.

### Pre-upgrade Collision Guard Design

After implementing the settings.json merge, add a general backstop:

In `copyManaged()` (or `brain-upgrade.mjs`), before overwriting a file:
1. Read both the incoming brain file content and the consumer's existing file content
2. If they differ AND the consumer file exists → log a warning with the path and the diff summary
3. Two modes: `--dry-run` (existing, reports but doesn't write), new `--warn-collisions` (writes but warns)
4. Optionally: compare against PREVIOUS brain version's known content to detect "consumer modified a managed file" (the real bad case)

Simplest viable guard: detect when `destFile` exists and differs from `srcFile`, log all such cases before copying starts (pre-flight report), then proceed unless `--abort-on-collision` is set.

### Approaches Comparison

| Approach | Pros | Cons | Effort |
|----------|------|------|--------|
| Slice 1: settings.json merge only | Stops catastrophe NOW, zero blast | scripts/ collision remains | Low |
| Slice 1+2: merge + collision guard | Safe foundation for future install | Still no namespace fix | Low-Medium |
| Slice 3: full scripts/ rename | Eliminates collision structurally | 74 critical refs, 87 files mv, breaking | High |
| Do nothing | Nothing breaks | Consumers get burned on first upgrade | None |

### Recommended Slice Breakdown (ordered)

**Slice 1 — settings.json MERGE** (1 PR, ~150 lines)
- `scripts/lib/installer.mjs`: add `mergeClaudeSettings()` function
- `scripts/brain-upgrade.mjs`: call merge for `.claude/settings.json` instead of raw copy
- `brain/core/managed-paths.mjs`: either remove `.claude/settings.json` from `managed` (and handle separately) OR keep in managed and gate on merge in the upgrade script
- New unit test for `mergeClaudeSettings()`
- Stops the 4.7KB allowlist destruction immediately

**Slice 2 — Pre-upgrade collision guard** (1 PR, ~100 lines)
- `scripts/lib/installer.mjs`: add pre-flight collision detection to `copyManaged()`
- `scripts/brain-upgrade.mjs`: surface warnings prominently; add `--abort-on-collision` flag
- Unit tests for guard logic
- Provides backstop for ALL managed paths, not just settings.json

**Slice 3 — scripts/ → brain/scripts/ RENAME** (1 PR, MUST be atomic)
- `git mv scripts/ brain/scripts/` (87 files)
- Update 74 critical references across 20 files
- Update ~15 test assertions
- CHANGELOG entry (breaking change, migration guide for existing consumers)
- Update README install instructions
- This commit MUST be a single atomic commit — no intermediate state

**Slice 4 — Synergy validation** (investigation + docs, not a code change)
- Read-only: run `brain:upgrade --dry-run` on synergy after slices 1-3
- Document NX monorepo integration notes
- Verify the 3-layer README diagram still makes sense

### Risks

1. **Self-hosting atomicity**: Brain's scripts run during development of brain. The rename must be done in a single coordinated commit — cannot rename incrementally while scripts are live. Risk: if the git mv + all references don't happen together, brain's own workflows break mid-rename.

2. **Existing consumer breaking change**: Any consumer that added brain's npm script aliases to their package.json pointing at `./scripts/bootstrap.sh` etc. must update those after upgrading to the renamed version. This is a hard breaking change requiring a migration guide in CHANGELOG.

3. **`core.hooksPath` one-time gap**: After upgrade, `core.hooksPath` still points to old `scripts/hooks`. The `day:start` self-heal checks `existsSync(brain/scripts/hooks/pre-push)` and finds it present (upgrade already copied files), then reconfigures. But ONLY if `brain:upgrade` ran BEFORE `day:start`. Order matters: `brain:upgrade` → `day:start` (or `env:init`).

4. **NX build cache pollution**: brain scripts landing at workspace root are not in any NX project's `projectRoot` so they don't affect per-project caching. Minor concern: NX affected detection might include `brain/scripts/**` in workspace-level changes, but this is benign.

5. **`node_modules/brain/brain/scripts/` path for initial bootstrap**: The README currently tells consumers to run `node node_modules/brain/scripts/brain-upgrade.mjs`. After rename it becomes `node node_modules/brain/brain/scripts/brain-upgrade.mjs`. The `brain/brain/` repetition looks odd but is technically correct. The test fixtures and container tests hardcode this path — all must be updated.

### Affected Files Summary

Critical path files:
- `/home/gandalf/IA/brain/brain/core/managed-paths.mjs` — managed list (both problems root here)
- `/home/gandalf/IA/brain/scripts/lib/installer.mjs` — copyManaged (plain overwrite, needs merge)
- `/home/gandalf/IA/brain/scripts/brain-upgrade.mjs` — orchestrates copy
- `/home/gandalf/IA/brain/scripts/day-start.mjs` — HOOKS_PATH constant + 3 spawn calls
- `/home/gandalf/IA/brain/scripts/bootstrap.sh` — core.hooksPath + 9 node calls
- `/home/gandalf/IA/brain/scripts/hooks/pre-push` — 4 node calls to scripts/
- `/home/gandalf/IA/brain/scripts/hooks/pre-commit` — 1 node call
- `/home/gandalf/IA/brain/scripts/hooks/post-merge` — 1 node call
- `/home/gandalf/IA/brain/.claude/settings.json` — 15-line hook block (what brain distributes)
- `/home/gandalf/IA/synergy/.claude/settings.json` — 4.7KB, 63 allow entries (what would be overwritten)

### Ready for Proposal
Yes — blast radius is fully mapped, both fixes are designed, slice ordering is clear.

content:
**sdd/issue-97-managed-paths/proposal**

## Proposal — Managed-Paths Namespace & Merge Safety (issue #97)

**Status:** Proposed. Artifact store HYBRID. File: openspec/changes/managed-paths-namespace/proposal.md

### Problem (two collisions, one root cause)
brain copies managed paths into a consumer on `brain:upgrade`. The list was authored against brain's own repo (all paths brain-owned); Docker install tests install into an EMPTY container so nothing ever collides — tests are green because the env has nothing to collide with. A real install into NX monorepo /home/gandalf/IA/synergy exposed two collisions:
- **A — `.claude/settings.json` OVERWRITTEN (catastrophic):** installer.mjs:copyManaged() does plain copyFileSync. brain's settings.json = 15 lines (only hooks.PreToolUse). synergy's = 4.7KB / 63 permissions.allow entries → silently destroyed. (settings.local.json survives — not managed.)
- **B — `scripts/**` claims consumer ROOT scripts/:** brain dumps 87 files into consumer's scripts/; shared filenames overwritten. Consumer's scripts/ no longer theirs.

Root cause: brain treats two consumer-owned surfaces as managed.

### Settled decisions
1. Namespace to **`brain/scripts/`** — completes 3-pillar model (brain/core managed methodology, brain/project consumer-owned, brain/scripts managed harness). Rejected `.brain/` (same blast, no benefit) and keep-scripts/ (no fix).
2. **MERGE settings.json** via `mergeClaudeSettings()` — additively insert brain's hooks.PreToolUse, deduped by serialized entry, preserve permissions.allow + other keys. Fresh consumer → brain's block as-is. settings.local.json untouched.
3. **Pre-upgrade collision guard** in copyManaged() + `--abort-on-collision` flag — general backstop so next undiscovered collision fails loud.

### 4 slices (chained PRs, feature-branch-chain, tracker feature/issue-97-managed-paths) — highest-value-first
- **S1 — settings.json MERGE (~150 lines):** STOPS DATA LOSS NOW. mergeClaudeSettings() in installer.mjs, wire in brain-upgrade.mjs, gate .claude/settings.json out of raw copy, tests (fixture w/ 63-entry allowlist proves no clobber). Independently shippable.
- **S2 — collision guard (~100 lines):** pre-flight diff check in copyManaged() + --abort-on-collision, tests.
- **S3 — scripts/ → brain/scripts/ ATOMIC rename:** git mv 87 files + ~74 critical refs across ~20 files (28 package.json aliases, bootstrap.sh core.hooksPath, day-start.mjs HOOKS_PATH, hooks, verbs, managed-paths.mjs, verify-change.mjs scope matcher, check-refs exempt lists, test-container scripts, 4 fixtures) + ~15 test assertions + README/CHANGELOG. MUST be SINGLE ATOMIC COMMIT (brain runs its own scripts; half-renamed state breaks brain). BREAKING.
- **S4 — synergy validation (docs/dry-run):** brain:upgrade --dry-run on synergy = zero collisions; NX integration notes; verify README 3-pillar diagram.

### Breaking change & migration (S3)
- Existing consumers keep orphaned root scripts/ after upgrade → installer never deletes; CHANGELOG MUST say "delete scripts/ after upgrading to vX.Y.Z".
- Alias path becomes node_modules/brain/brain/scripts/... (double brain/, intentional).
- Warrants minor/major bump — number deferred to release.
- **core.hooksPath one-time window:** per-clone, not committed. After upgrade but before next env:init/day:start, hooks point at stale scripts/hooks → self-heals on next day:start. Required order: brain:upgrade → day:start/env:init. Document in upgrade notes.

### Out of scope
Deeper NX integration beyond dry-run (follow-up); .brain/ alternate; auto-delete orphaned scripts/; touching settings.local.json; other managed paths.

### Rollback
S1 additive, revert function+wiring. S2 read-only+opt-in flag. S3 revert single atomic commit (clean, no half-state). S4 docs only.

### ADR note
Alters managed-paths model + establishes merge-don't-overwrite rule → likely **ADR-0006 update** (or new ADR) covering the 3-pillar core/project/scripts split + merge-vs-overwrite policy. Author alongside S3. This proposal flags it, does not author it.

### Risks / open questions
- Self-hosting atomicity of S3 (the dominant risk).
- Exact version number (deferred).
- core.hooksPath transition gap (documented, acceptable).

**next_recommended:** sdd-spec + sdd-design (parallel).

content:
**sdd/issue-97-managed-paths/design**

# Design: Managed-Paths Namespace & Merge Safety (issue #97)

HYBRID artifact. File: openspec/changes/managed-paths-namespace/design.md

## Technical Approach
Two consumer-owned surfaces wrongly `managed` and clobbered on brain:upgrade: `.claude/settings.json` (plain copyFileSync overwrite) + root `scripts/**`. Three composable mechanisms across 4 chained PRs (feature-branch-chain, tracker feature/issue-97-managed-paths): S1 merge settings.json, S2 collision guard, S3 atomic scripts/→brain/scripts/ rename, S4 synergy dry-run docs. Merge and guard composable: settings.json takes merge path, EXCLUDED from collision guard.

## Decisions
1. **specialMerge map in installer API (RECOMMENDED over alternatives)**: copyManaged() gains `specialMerge: {relPath → mergeFn}`. .claude/settings.json STAYS in managed (still distributed + drift-checked) but routes through mergeClaudeSettings instead of copyFileSync. Keeps copy/skip/merge policy in ONE unit-testable place, mirrors mergeDefaults/migrateConfig seam, generalizes. Rejected: (a) remove from managed + bespoke post-copy merge in brain-upgrade.mjs (scatters policy, breaks drift guard); (b) raw copy (the bug). dry-run reports `merged` distinct from `copied`.
2. **mergeClaudeSettings(existingPath, brainSettingsPath) mirrors mergeDefaults**: fresh consumer (no dest)→write brain's as-is; existing→spread consumer obj, additively append brain's hooks.PreToolUse deduped by JSON.stringify, preserve permissions.allow + all other keys. brain only OWNS PreToolUse block (ADR-0014 §9). Existing-value-wins invariant. Idempotent.
3. **Collision guard**: in copyManaged(), pre-write, for each managed file NOT a specialMerge target: dest exists AND bytes differ from src → collect. Return collisions[]. brain:upgrade surfaces report; --abort-on-collision throws BEFORE any write (all-or-nothing, clean rollback). Default: warn+proceed (current behavior). settings.json excluded (uses merge).
4. **S3 ONE atomic commit**: git mv scripts/→brain/scripts/ (87 files) + all refs + test assertions in SINGLE commit. Order: git mv → rewrite refs → run suite + real dry-run → commit. WHY: self-hosting — brain runs its own scripts/ (hooks, day:start, governance workflow); half-renamed state breaks brain's own tooling (core.hooksPath, aliases, inter-script calls). Revert = one clean commit.
5. **core.hooksPath transition**: NOT migrated by installer (per-clone, not committed). Documented order brain:upgrade→day:start/env:init. day:start self-heals once HOOKS_PATH constant = brain/scripts/hooks. One-time stale window acceptable, documented.

## S3 Reference MAP (~74 refs / ~20 files)
- package.json aliases (28): ./scripts/X → ./brain/scripts/X; test glob scripts/**/*.test.mjs → brain/scripts/**/*.test.mjs
- Bootstrap install alias: node_modules/brain/scripts/... → node_modules/brain/brain/scripts/... (double brain/, intentional)
- core.hooksPath (THE coupling): scripts/hooks → brain/scripts/hooks in bootstrap.sh §7 (git config core.hooksPath) + day-start.mjs `const HOOKS_PATH`
- managed-paths.mjs: 'scripts/**' → 'brain/scripts/**'
- Inter-script calls: $repo_root/scripts/..., node scripts/... → brain/scripts/... in bootstrap.sh (~9), day-start.mjs (4 spawn), install-tools.sh, brain-save.mjs, brain-next.mjs, memory/lib/auto-resume.mjs
- Hooks: $repo_root/scripts/... → brain/scripts/... in pre-push(4), pre-commit(1), post-merge(1)
- Scope matcher: f.startsWith('scripts/') → 'brain/scripts/' in verify-change.mjs(2), check-refs-rules.mjs exempt(2), check-refs.mjs exempt(1)
- Governance workflow (.github/workflows/governance.yml, MANAGED): `node scripts/vcs/diff-size-count.mjs` → brain/scripts/... (~line76) + header comment scripts/vcs/governance-checks.mjs (~line5)
- Governance drift-guard: NO change — scripts/vcs/governance-checks.mjs syncs JOB NAMES not script paths (verified, audit only)
- Test container scripts (8): test/fresh-install/in-container.sh, test/upgrade/in-container.sh
- Test fixtures (8/4 files): test/fixtures/*/package.json aliases
- Test assertions (~15): installer.test.mjs(5), check-refs.test.mjs(3), diff-size*.test.mjs(4), i18n/coverage.test.mjs(2), managed-paths.test.mjs(1)
- Docs (~85 non-breaking): README, openspec docs, docs/inbox, source headers

## Interfaces
mergeClaudeSettings(existingPath, brainSettingsPath) → object
copyManaged opts: + specialMerge?: Record<relPath, (destPath,srcPath)=>object>
copyManaged result: + { merged: string[], collisions: string[] }

## Migration
Existing consumers keep orphaned root scripts/ (installer never deletes) → CHANGELOG MUST say delete scripts/ after upgrade. Version bump deferred. hooksPath self-heals. Rollback: S1 additive, S2 read-only/opt-in, S3 revert one atomic commit, S4 docs.

## ADR Note (DEFER to S3)
S3 alters managed-paths model (adds brain/scripts/ namespace, completes 3-pillar core/project/scripts) + establishes merge-don't-overwrite for managed config → warrants ADR-0006 update, authored IN S3, NOT here. Design only flags it.

## Out of Scope
Deeper NX integration beyond S4 dry-run (follow-up); .brain/ alt; auto-delete orphaned scripts/; settings.local.json; other managed paths.

## Open Questions
- Exact version bump (minor/major) — deferred to release.
- Whether specialMerge feeds dry-run "would merge" content diff (nice-to-have).

content:
**sdd/issue-97-managed-paths/spec**

# Managed-Paths Namespace & Merge Safety Specification

## Purpose

Eliminates two silent data-loss collisions in `brain:upgrade` — settings.json overwrite
and scripts/ namespace takeover — by introducing a merge strategy for shared consumer
config, a general pre-upgrade collision guard, and a `brain/scripts/` namespace for the
managed harness. The change completes brain's 3-pillar model: `brain/core` (managed
methodology), `brain/project` (consumer-owned overrides), `brain/scripts` (managed harness
verbs). The consumer's root `scripts/` is restored to consumer ownership.

## Epic Invariant

`brain:upgrade` MUST NOT overwrite consumer-owned content. Every path in the `managed`
array MUST be either (a) exclusively brain-owned, or (b) merged additively so consumer
keys are preserved and provably intact after every upgrade.

## Requirement Index

| Req | Slice | Name | Testable |
|-----|-------|------|---------|
| REQ-S1-1 | 1 | mergeClaudeSettings — fresh consumer | Unit (`node --test`) |
| REQ-S1-2 | 1 | mergeClaudeSettings — existing consumer, no clobber | Unit (`node --test`) |
| REQ-S1-3 | 1 | mergeClaudeSettings — idempotent re-run | Unit (`node --test`) |
| REQ-S1-4 | 1 | settings.local.json never touched | Unit (`node --test`) |
| REQ-S1-5 | 1 | .claude/settings.json excluded from raw copyManaged | Unit (`node --test`) |
| REQ-S2-1 | 2 | Pre-flight collision report | Unit (`node --test`) |
| REQ-S2-2 | 2 | --abort-on-collision aborts before any write | Unit (`node --test`) |
| REQ-S2-3 | 2 | No false positive when dest is identical or absent | Unit (`node --test`) |
| REQ-S3-1 | 3 | managed-paths declares brain/scripts/**, not scripts/** | Unit (`node --test`) |
| REQ-S3-2 | 3 | All runtime references resolve under brain/scripts/ | Full suite + integration |
| REQ-S3-3 | 3 | Consumer root scripts/ is not a managed path | Unit (`node --test`) |
| REQ-S3-4 | 3 | CHANGELOG documents breaking change and migration | File assertion |
| REQ-S3-5 | 3 | core.hooksPath transition window documented | File assertion |
| REQ-S3-6 | 3 | Single atomic commit delivers the rename | Git history assertion |
| REQ-S4-1 | 4 | brain:upgrade --dry-run on synergy: zero collisions | Manual / dry-run |
| REQ-E-1 | epic | brain:upgrade never overwrites consumer-owned content | Verified by S1+S2+S3 |

---

## Slice 1 — `.claude/settings.json` MERGE

### REQ-S1-1: Fresh consumer

#### Scenario: No existing settings.json — brain block written
- GIVEN no `.claude/settings.json` exists in the consumer repo
- WHEN `brain:upgrade` runs (via `mergeClaudeSettings()`)
- THEN the consumer's `.claude/settings.json` is created with brain's `hooks.PreToolUse` block
- AND its content is byte-for-byte identical to brain's 15-line settings block

### REQ-S1-2: Existing consumer, no clobber

#### Scenario: 63-entry permissions.allow survives upgrade
- GIVEN a consumer `.claude/settings.json` with 63 `permissions.allow` entries
- WHEN `brain:upgrade` runs
- THEN the result file contains all 63 original `permissions.allow` entries
- AND brain's `hooks.PreToolUse` entries are present
- AND no consumer key is absent from the merged result

#### Scenario: Consumer hooks not owned by brain are preserved
- GIVEN a consumer `.claude/settings.json` with a custom `hooks.PreToolUse` entry not in brain's block
- WHEN `brain:upgrade` runs
- THEN the custom entry is still present alongside brain's entries

### REQ-S1-3: Idempotent

#### Scenario: Second upgrade adds nothing
- GIVEN `brain:upgrade` has already merged brain's `hooks.PreToolUse` into the consumer
- WHEN `brain:upgrade` runs a second time
- THEN the `.claude/settings.json` is byte-for-byte identical to the post-first-run result
- AND no brain `hooks.PreToolUse` entry appears more than once

### REQ-S1-4: settings.local.json never touched

#### Scenario: settings.local.json is byte-identical before and after upgrade
- GIVEN a consumer `settings.local.json` with arbitrary content
- WHEN `brain:upgrade` runs
- THEN `settings.local.json` is byte-for-byte identical to its pre-upgrade state

### REQ-S1-5: .claude/settings.json excluded from raw copyManaged

#### Scenario: Raw copyManaged never writes settings.json
- GIVEN a consumer with an existing `.claude/settings.json`
- WHEN `copyManaged()` iterates managed paths
- THEN `.claude/settings.json` is NOT written via `copyFileSync` at any point during the run

---

## Slice 2 — Pre-Upgrade Collision Guard

### REQ-S2-1: Pre-flight collision report

#### Scenario: Differing managed file produces collision record
- GIVEN a managed path whose destination exists with content different from the incoming brain file
- WHEN `brain:upgrade` runs the pre-flight check
- THEN the collision is recorded AND the report is printed before any file is written

#### Scenario: Collision report printed before first write
- GIVEN two managed paths have collisions
- WHEN `brain:upgrade` runs
- THEN both collision paths appear in the pre-flight output before no managed file is overwritten

### REQ-S2-2: --abort-on-collision

#### Scenario: --abort-on-collision aborts before any write
- GIVEN a managed path whose destination differs from brain's version
- WHEN `brain:upgrade --abort-on-collision` runs
- THEN the command exits non-zero AND no managed file is written to disk

#### Scenario: No collision — proceeds normally
- GIVEN all managed path destinations are absent or identical to brain's versions
- WHEN `brain:upgrade --abort-on-collision` runs
- THEN the command exits zero and writes proceed normally

### REQ-S2-3: No false positive

#### Scenario: Absent destination is not a collision
- GIVEN a managed path whose destination does not exist
- WHEN the pre-flight check runs
- THEN no collision is recorded for that path

#### Scenario: Identical destination is not a collision
- GIVEN a managed path whose destination is byte-for-byte identical to the brain source
- WHEN the pre-flight check runs
- THEN no collision is recorded for that path

---

## Slice 3 — `scripts/` → `brain/scripts/` Atomic Rename (BREAKING)

### REQ-S3-1: managed-paths declares brain/scripts/**

#### Scenario: managed array contains brain/scripts/**
- GIVEN S3 has merged
- WHEN the `managed` export from `managed-paths.mjs` is inspected
- THEN `'brain/scripts/**'` is present AND `'scripts/**'` is absent

### REQ-S3-2: All runtime references resolve under brain/scripts/

#### Scenario: npm test green after rename
- GIVEN S3 has merged as a single atomic commit
- WHEN `npm test` runs in the brain repo
- THEN all tests exit zero

#### Scenario: hooks, verbs, and bootstrap resolve correctly
- GIVEN `bootstrap.sh` and `day-start.mjs` have been updated
- WHEN `core.hooksPath` is inspected after a fresh `env:init`
- THEN it resolves to `brain/scripts/hooks`

#### Scenario: Fresh-install integration test passes
- GIVEN a clean Docker test container
- WHEN the fresh-install integration test runs
- THEN the install succeeds with no broken path references

#### Scenario: Upgrade integration test passes
- GIVEN a Docker test container with a previous brain installation
- WHEN the upgrade integration test runs
- THEN `brain:upgrade` completes with all files under `brain/scripts/`

### REQ-S3-3: Consumer root scripts/ is not a managed path

#### Scenario: Consumer file under root scripts/ survives upgrade
- GIVEN a consumer with a file at `scripts/consumer-sentinel.sh`
- WHEN `brain:upgrade` runs after S3 merges
- THEN `scripts/consumer-sentinel.sh` is byte-for-byte identical to its pre-upgrade state
- AND no brain file is written under root `scripts/`

### REQ-S3-4: CHANGELOG documents breaking change

#### Scenario: CHANGELOG contains orphaned-scripts cleanup instruction
- GIVEN S3 has merged
- WHEN `CHANGELOG.md` is read
- THEN it contains an instruction to delete root `scripts/` after upgrading
- AND it references the new bootstrap path `node_modules/brain/brain/scripts/`
- AND it specifies the `brain:upgrade` → `day:start`/`env:init` required order

### REQ-S3-5: core.hooksPath transition window documented

#### Scenario: core.hooksPath transition gap is documented
- GIVEN S3 has merged
- WHEN the CHANGELOG or upgrade notes are read
- THEN the text explains that `core.hooksPath` points to the old path until `day:start` self-heals
- AND the mitigation order (`brain:upgrade` then `day:start`) is explicitly stated

### REQ-S3-6: Single atomic commit

#### Scenario: One commit contains move and all reference updates
- GIVEN S3 has merged
- WHEN `git show <S3-commit-sha> --stat` is run
- THEN both moved `brain/scripts/` files and updated references appear in the same commit
- AND no prior commit in the S3 branch contains a partial rename

---

## Slice 4 — Synergy Validation

### REQ-S4-1: brain:upgrade --dry-run on synergy reports zero collisions

#### Scenario: Dry-run on synergy reports zero collisions
- GIVEN S1, S2, and S3 have merged
- WHEN `brain:upgrade --dry-run` is run targeting `/home/gandalf/IA/synergy`
- THEN the output contains zero collision records AND the command exits zero

#### Scenario: NX integration notes written
- GIVEN S4 is complete
- WHEN the brain docs are inspected
- THEN NX integration notes exist covering workspace-root coexistence, `namedInputs` non-interference, and root `package.json` alias coexistence

---

## Epic Requirements

### REQ-E-1: brain:upgrade MUST NOT Overwrite Consumer-Owned Content

#### Scenario: Upgrade on synergy consumer produces no data loss
- GIVEN synergy with its 63-entry `permissions.allow` and no root `scripts/` dir
- WHEN `brain:upgrade` runs after S1–S3 merge
- THEN `synergy/.claude/settings.json` retains all 63 `permissions.allow` entries
- AND brain's `hooks.PreToolUse` block is present
- AND no file under synergy's workspace is unexpectedly overwritten

---

## Gaps and Assumptions

| # | Gap / Assumption |
|---|-----------------|
| G1 | Integration tests (REQ-S3-2) require Docker or equivalent isolated environment; not coverable by `node --test` alone. |
| G2 | Version bump number for S3 breaking change deferred to release time. |
| G3 | S4 dry-run is manual acceptance, not automated. |
| G4 | Orphaned root scripts/ cleanup is a documented manual step; brain:upgrade MUST NOT auto-delete consumer files. |
| G5 | ADR update (ADR-0006 or new ADR) covering 3-pillar model and merge-vs-overwrite policy is flagged by proposal and deferred to S3 authorship. |

# Tasks: Managed-Paths Namespace & Merge Safety (issue #97)

## Review Workload Forecast

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Estimated changed lines | S1 ~150 · S2 ~100 · S3 ~700 (mechanical) · S4 ~50 |
| 400-line budget risk | High |
| Chained PRs recommended | Yes |
| Suggested split | PR1 (S1) → PR2 (S2) → PR3 (S3, size:exception) → PR4 (S4) |
| Delivery strategy | feature-branch-chain |
| Chain strategy | feature-branch-chain |

Decision needed before apply: No
Chained PRs recommended: Yes
Chain strategy: feature-branch-chain
400-line budget risk: High

S3 size:exception: 87 file moves + ~74 refs cannot split — partial state breaks brain's self-hosted tooling simultaneously. Revert = one clean commit. Reviewer load is mechanical (search/replace + git mv).

### Suggested Work Units

| Unit | Goal | PR base | Notes |
|------|------|---------|-------|
| S1 | mergeClaudeSettings + wiring | feature/issue-97-managed-paths (tracker) | TDD; independently shippable |
| S2 | Collision guard + --abort-on-collision | S1 branch | TDD; independently shippable |
| S3 | Atomic scripts/ → brain/scripts/ rename | S2 branch | ONE commit; size:exception |
| S4 | Synergy dry-run + NX docs | S3 branch | Manual acceptance gate |

## S1 — settings.json Merge (TDD) [REQ-S1-1..5]

- [ ] 1.1 [RED] scripts/lib/installer.test.mjs: failing tests for mergeClaudeSettings() — fresh, 63-entry preserved, custom hook preserved, idempotent, settings.local.json absent from managed, settings.json excluded from raw copy. npm test → RED.
- [ ] 1.2 [GREEN] scripts/lib/installer.mjs: mergeClaudeSettings(existingPath, brainSettingsPath) — no dest → write as-is; dest exists → spread consumer obj + append brain's hooks.PreToolUse deduped by JSON.stringify + preserve all keys. npm test → GREEN.
- [ ] 1.3 scripts/lib/installer.mjs copyManaged(): add specialMerge?: Record<relPath, mergeFn> param; match → call mergeFn not copyFileSync; add merged: string[] to result; --dry-run labels as merged.
- [ ] 1.4 scripts/brain-upgrade.mjs: pass specialMerge: { '.claude/settings.json': mergeClaudeSettings }; surface merged[] and collisions[] in output.
- [ ] 1.5 npm test → zero. Open PR1 → feature/issue-97-managed-paths.

## S2 — Collision Guard (TDD) [REQ-S2-1..3]

- [ ] 2.1 [RED] scripts/lib/installer.test.mjs: collision when dest≠src, report before first write, abort exits non-zero + zero writes, no-op when clean, absent/identical = no collision, specialMerge excluded. npm test → RED.
- [ ] 2.2 [GREEN] scripts/lib/installer.mjs copyManaged(): before any write, each managed file NOT in specialMerge — if dest exists AND bytes differ → push collisions[]; return collisions: string[].
- [ ] 2.3 scripts/brain-upgrade.mjs: parse --abort-on-collision; if collisions + flag → print report, exit(1), no writes; default → warn + proceed.
- [ ] 2.4 npm test → zero. Open PR2 → PR1 branch.

## S3 — Atomic Rename (ONE commit) [REQ-S3-1..6]

Stage 3.1–3.8, verify gates 3.9–3.10, ONE commit (3.11). No intermediate commits.

- [ ] 3.1 git mv scripts/ brain/scripts/ — stage 87 file moves.
- [ ] 3.2 Design §MAP ~74 refs / ~20 files: package.json 28 aliases + test glob; bootstrap.sh §7 + day-start.mjs HOOKS_PATH (scripts/hooks → brain/scripts/hooks); managed-paths.mjs 'scripts/**' → 'brain/scripts/**'; inter-script calls in bootstrap.sh(~9)+day-start.mjs(4)+install-tools.sh+brain-save.mjs+brain-next.mjs+auto-resume.mjs; hooks pre-push(4)+pre-commit(1)+post-merge(1); scope matchers verify-change.mjs(2)+check-refs-rules.mjs(2)+check-refs.mjs(1); governance.yml line~76 + comment line~5.
- [ ] 3.3 Bootstrap install alias in README + fixtures + container scripts: node_modules/brain/scripts/ → node_modules/brain/brain/scripts/ (double brain/, intentional).
- [ ] 3.4 test/fresh-install/in-container.sh + test/upgrade/in-container.sh: 8 path refs → brain/scripts/.
- [ ] 3.5 test/fixtures/{npm,pnpm,bun,yarn}/package.json: 8 alias entries → ./brain/scripts/.
- [ ] 3.6 Test assertions (~15): installer.test.mjs(5), check-refs.test.mjs(3), diff-size*.test.mjs(4), i18n/coverage.test.mjs(2), managed-paths.test.mjs(1). Plus README + openspec/ docs + docs/inbox headers (~85 doc refs).
- [ ] 3.7 CHANGELOG.md: breaking entry — (a) delete orphaned root scripts/; (b) new bootstrap path node_modules/brain/brain/scripts/brain-upgrade.mjs; (c) order brain:upgrade → day:start/env:init; (d) core.hooksPath stale-window + self-heal. [REQ-S3-4, REQ-S3-5]
- [ ] 3.8 ADR-0006 update under docs/decisions/: 3-pillar brain/core+brain/project+brain/scripts model, brain/scripts/ namespace, merge-don't-overwrite policy.
- [ ] 3.9 Gate: npm test + npm run repo:check + npm run brain:nav → all zero.
- [ ] 3.10 Gate: Docker test/fresh-install/in-container.sh + test/upgrade/in-container.sh → pass. [REQ-S3-2 integration]
- [ ] 3.11 ONE git commit all staged (3.1–3.8). git show HEAD --stat must list both renamed files and ref updates. Open PR3 → PR2 branch; label size:exception. [REQ-S3-6]

## S4 — Synergy Validation (manual) [REQ-S4-1, REQ-E-1]

- [ ] 4.1 brain:upgrade --dry-run --cwd /home/gandalf/IA/synergy → zero collision records, exit zero.
- [ ] 4.2 docs/inbox/nx-coexistence.md: workspace-root non-interference, namedInputs non-interference, package.json alias coexistence.
- [ ] 4.3 Verify README 3-pillar diagram: brain/core + brain/project + brain/scripts.
- [ ] 4.4 Epic closure (REQ-E-1): synergy 63-entry permissions.allow intact + zero unexpected overwrites. Open PR4 → PR3 branch.

## Closure Checklist

- [ ] Epic invariant: no consumer-owned file overwritten via copyFileSync (verified by 1.2 + 2.2 + 3.5).
- [ ] Synergy dry-run (4.1) = zero collisions.
- [ ] PR chain: PR1 → PR2 → PR3 (size:exception) → PR4 → tracker merges to main.

Version-bump: S3 is BREAKING. Semver (minor/major) deferred to release.

**Session summary: brain**

## HANDOFF para el agente de mañana (otra PC) — estado completo

## Goal de la sesión
Construir la capa de gobernanza de brain, releasearla, y dejarla instalable en proyectos reales. Terminó exponiendo un blocker real (managed-paths colisionan) ahora planeado.

## Estado actual (main, v0.6.1)
- **Gobernanza COMPLETA** (epic #67, en main): floor (checks genéricos + hooks reliability-first + brain:audit) + adapter capability-aware (brain:protect/governance-status) + golden path (brain:start/check/save/ship/next) + política --no-verify. ADR-0014.
- **brain instala via npm Y pnpm** (v0.6.1, validado en Docker). pnpm necesitó: remover `prepare` de package.json (#86) + sacar packageManager pins de los fixtures (#89).
- **Hooks**: core.hooksPath es per-clon (no committeado); brain:upgrade trae los archivos, env:init/day:start configuran (day:start self-healea). El audit es la red universal (no depende de config local).

## EL BLOCKER que synergy expuso (ahora planeado — #97)
Instalar brain en /home/gandalf/IA/synergy (NX monorepo real) **pisaría**: (a) su `.claude/settings.json` con 63 permisos (brain lo sobreescribe con su hook de 15 líneas — pérdida de datos); (b) volcaría 87 scripts en su `scripts/`. Los tests Docker no lo veían (consumers vacíos).
- **Plan landeado** (tracker `feature/managed-paths-namespace`, PR #98 draft, openspec proposal/spec/design/tasks): S1 merge de settings.json (frena la pérdida YA) → S2 collision guard → S3 rename ATÓMICO scripts/→brain/scripts/ (BREAKING, 87 files, 74 refs, un commit, size:exception) + update ADR-0006 → S4 validar en synergy (dry-run = 0 colisiones). El mapa de las 74 refs está en design.md.
- **NO instalar brain en synergy hasta que #97 esté.**

## Tickets para tomar (todos status:approved, self-contained)
- **#91** [bug, 🟢 PRIMERO] commit-msg exime merge/revert/release/memory de #N (la regla actual es muy agresiva → lleva a --no-verify).
- **#93** [feat] brain:audit honra size:exception + baseline.
- **#92** [feat] audit/gate scope — Part-of vs Closes (chained-PR semantics).
- **#94** [chore, 🔴 NECESITA decisión humana de tier] activar brain:protect (repo privado+free da 403; necesita Pro o público) + coordinar branch i18n.
- **#95** [feat, EPIC] Phase 3 — GitLab/Bitbucket/self-hosted branchProtect + pre-receive.
- **#96** [feat, spike] Phase 3 — memory check completo (session_summary) — spike del schema engram + convención session/{issue}.
- **#97** [bug, EPIC, plan landeado] el de managed-paths arriba.

## Cómo retomar en PC2
git pull → npm run memory:pull → npm run day:start (muestra los tickets) → npm run brain:start -- <N>. Para #97/#95/#97 epics: checkout del tracker correspondiente para el openspec.

## Refs clave
- managed-paths: brain/core/managed-paths.mjs, scripts/lib/installer.mjs (copyManaged + el specialMerge a agregar)
- gobernanza: scripts/governance/checks/, scripts/brain-audit.mjs, scripts/hooks/, .claude/settings.json
- ADR-0014 (gobernanza), ADR-0006 (a actualizar en #97 S3)
- docs/inbox/workflow-governance-layer.md (design v2)

content:
**Fixed synergy half-adoption — wired brain v0.7.0 vendored harness**

**What**: Made the synergy consumer repo (csrinaldi/synergy, /home/gandalf/IA/synergy) operational as a brain v0.7.0 vendored consumer after a half-adoption left it broken.

**Why**: `pnpm run brain:upgrade -- v0.7.2` failed with `Missing script: brain:upgrade`. Root cause: brain was adopted by VENDORING the `brain/` dir (commit ed791c3 "adopt brain v0.7.0") but nothing wired it — no `brain:*` scripts in package.json, no brain.config.json, no manifest, brain not in node_modules. brain:adopt S1 (issue #121, the only slice shipped) is READ-ONLY inventory (writes .brain-adopt/ only); the apply/wire/env:init slice was never built, so #121 was effectively closed with scope reduced to inventory. synergy is literally the case study in #121's body ("brain:upgrade alone doesn't configure anything... bare brain:upgrade is half-adoption").

**Where (synergy, brain repo untouched)**:
- package.json — added canonical brain verbs pointing to ./brain/scripts/*.mjs (brain:upgrade/start/check/save/ship/next/nav/protect/protect-server/governance-status/audit + day:start, ticket:start, project:feature, project:status, tracker:board, repo:check, change:verify, memory:*, feature:*, env:init). Excluded brain:adopt (adopt.mjs MISSING in vendored copy), test* (would shadow NX), tools:install (namespace clutter).
- brain.config.json — created via `node brain/scripts/lib/brain-config.mjs ensure` → github / csrinaldi/synergy / schemaVersion 0.4.0.

**Learned**: 
- brain:check/brain:start REQUIRE brain.config.json (brain-check.mjs:38, brain-start.mjs:101).
- After wiring, brain:check RUNS but reports content-level FAILs (issueLink, memoryPresence, npmTest misfire on pnpm/nx, repoCheck "Active change missing tasks.md") — NOT wiring issues. The repoCheck failure is the pre-existing prescriptions-management openspec change missing tasks.md, the exact structural gate #121 names.
- brain:upgrade even in vendored mode pulls the tag into node_modules/brain then copies managed paths over the vendored brain/ — needs authenticated access to the PRIVATE brain repo. Decision: leave synergy at v0.7.0; bump to v0.7.2 coordinated with the other agent running the adopt. Changes left UNCOMMITTED.

content:
**Started SDD issue-138 session:start (universal context loader)**

**What**: Kicked off SDD change `issue-138-session-start` in brain — a universal, agent-agnostic `session:start` context loader, distinct from the heavy/manual `day:start`.

**Why**: `day:start` (brain/scripts/day-start.mjs) is networked and manual; it does NOT survive context compaction, so a resuming agent loses brain's operational context. Need a fast, read-only, LOCAL-ONLY entry point that runs on every startup/resume/compact.

**Agreed design (load-bearing)**:
- Agent-agnostic CORE: `brain/scripts/session-start.mjs`, run via `npm run session:start`. Any agent/human invokes it. Mirrors brain's tool-agnostic governance (universal floor + thin per-platform adapter).
- Claude Code adapter = thin `SessionStart` hook in `.claude/settings.json` (managed path) that only runs `npm run session:start`. No other-agent adapters now (YAGNI); document the seam. User explicitly flagged: do NOT couple brain's context loading to Claude.
- NO NETWORK on this path. `memory/cli.mjs pull` is the ONLY networked memory op — never call it here. `import`/`feature-resume`/`feature-checkpoint` are local-only and safe.
- Steps: restore .memory/manifest.json churn → `memory/cli.mjs import` (hydrate local engram) → NEW `deriveChangeFromBranch()` (match `issue-<N>` token to openspec/changes/*) → EXISTING `tryFeatureResume()` (resume.md → next_action/blockers) → print compact context to stdout.
- Abstract shared logic from day-start: `lib/git-branch.mjs` (current-branch, dup at day-start.mjs:150 + engram.mjs:296) and `lib/memory-manifest.mjs` (restore, inline day-start.mjs:117-129). day:start becomes a consumer.

**Process**: SDD issue-first. GitHub issue #138 created (type:feature, awaiting human status:approved). Routing via gentle-ai dispatcher (`gentle-ai sdd-continue issue-138-session-start`), artifact gen via sdd-* sub-agents. Artifact store hybrid, strict TDD on (`npm test`). Builds on already-merged `feature-working-memory` (resume.md infra).

**Where**: openspec/changes/issue-138-session-start/, brain/scripts/ (new session-start.mjs + libs), .claude/settings.json (adapter), brain/scripts/i18n/{en,es}.mjs (session.* keys).

**sdd/issue-138-session-start/proposal**

**What**: SDD proposal for issue #138 — `session:start`, a universal, read-only, LOCAL-ONLY session context loader plus a thin Claude Code adapter.

**Why**: `day:start` is heavy/manual/networked (run once/day) and does NOT survive context compaction; resuming agents lose brain's operational context. No fast automatic "load my brain context now" entry point exists.

**Where**: openspec/changes/issue-138-session-start/proposal.md (hybrid). New: brain/scripts/session-start.mjs, brain/scripts/lib/git-branch.mjs, brain/scripts/lib/memory-manifest.mjs. Modified: brain/scripts/day-start.mjs, .claude/settings.json (SessionStart hook, managed-path), brain/scripts/i18n/en.mjs + es.mjs, package.json (session:start).

**Scope (in)**: (a) restore `.memory/manifest.json` churn; (b) hydrate local engram via `memory/cli.mjs import` (local); (c) NEW `deriveChangeFromBranch()` mapping `issue-<N>` token → openspec/changes/*; (d) load active-ticket memory via EXISTING `tryFeatureResume()`; (e) print compact context block. Extract shared libs git-branch + memory-manifest; day:start becomes a consumer. Add `session.*` i18n keys (en canonical + es) with colocated tests.

**Scope (out / non-goals)**: NO network on session-start path — `memory/cli.mjs pull` MUST NEVER run here (only networked memory op). No export-on-compact / feature-checkpoint writes (read-only path). No Cursor/Cline/Codex adapters now (YAGNI — document the seam). No change to day:start networked workflow beyond consuming new libs.

**Capabilities**: New `session-start`. Modified: None (day:start refactor is implementation-level lib reuse).

**Approach**: Agent-agnostic core (universal floor in session-start.mjs) + thin optional per-agent adapter (Claude Code SessionStart hook), mirroring brain's existing tool-agnostic governance. Reuse only verified-local ops. Build the missing branch→openspec mapping. Extract duplicated current-branch (day-start.mjs:150, memory/backends/engram.mjs:296) and manifest-restore (day-start.mjs:117-129) logic.

**Learned**: Builds on merged `feature-working-memory` (resume.md / feature-resume infra). Strict TDD ON (npm test, node:test, colocated *.test.mjs). ADR-0002: repo owns SDD structure; gentle-ai is router. Key risk: accidental network op on hot path — mitigate with local-op allowlist + test asserting no network. No existing openspec/specs/, so session-start is a brand-new capability.

**Status**: done. Next: sdd-spec and sdd-design (parallelizable).

content:
**Follow-up: governance should enforce SDD phase contract (harness-agnostic)**

**What**: Identified a real governance gap to address AFTER issue-138 lands. The agent had to be TOLD to route SDD via gentle-ai — nothing enforces or guarantees it.

**Why**: User asked whether "use gentle-ai" is part of brain's governance. Verified: it is NOT.
- `brain/core/methodology/harness-contract.md:8` defines gentle-ai as the CURRENT, REPLACEABLE harness (abstract verbs; ADR-0001/0002 = 3-layer harness-replaceable architecture). It is a contract, not a gate.
- Governance enforcement (`brain-protect*.mjs`, `check-refs.mjs`, `.github/workflows/governance.yml`) does NOT reference gentle-ai at all — routing via gentle-ai is NOT a fail-closed gate.
- The "use gentle-ai dispatcher" instruction lives in the USER's global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (orchestrator config), not in brain.

**Design tension (important)**: brain is DELIBERATELY harness-neutral. Enforcing "you MUST use gentle-ai" would CONTRADICT ADR-0001/0002. So the fix is NOT to mandate gentle-ai. The correct fix is to enforce the ABSTRACT phase contract harness-agnostically: that an SDD change has proposal→spec→design→tasks→verify present and in dependency order, whoever routes it.

**Decision**: Park this as a SEPARATE governance change (own issue), tackled after issue-138-session-start. Do NOT mix scope into #138.

**Where**: brain/core/methodology/harness-contract.md, brain/scripts/brain-protect*.mjs, .github/workflows/governance.yml, brain/project/decisions/adr-0001/adr-0002.

content:
**sdd/issue-138-session-start/spec**

# Session Start Specification

## Purpose

Defines the observable behavior of `session:start` — a universal, read-only,
local-only context loader that any agent or human can run to restore brain's
operational context (manifest, engram, active change, ticket memory) without
the cost or network surface of `day:start`.

## Requirements

### Requirement: REQ-1 Universal Invocation

The system MUST expose `npm run session:start` as an agent-agnostic entry
point. The core script MUST NOT contain Claude-specific (or any other
agent-specific) logic.

#### Scenario: Run from any shell context
- GIVEN a clean checkout of the `brain` repo
- WHEN any agent or human runs `npm run session:start`
- THEN the process exits with code 0 and prints a context block

### Requirement: REQ-2 Local-Only / No-Network Invariant

The system MUST perform ZERO network calls on the `session:start` hot path.
`memory/cli.mjs pull` MUST NEVER be invoked from this path. Only local
operations from the allowlist (`memory/cli.mjs import`, `feature-resume`-class
reads) are permitted.

#### Scenario: No network calls during execution
- GIVEN network access is disabled (e.g. via a network-stub/sandbox in tests)
- WHEN `npm run session:start` runs
- THEN it completes successfully with no network I/O attempted

#### Scenario: pull is never invoked
- GIVEN `session-start.mjs` source and its dependency graph
- WHEN inspected/tested
- THEN no code path calls `memory/cli.mjs pull` or any non-allowlisted op

### Requirement: REQ-3 Manifest Restore Before Other Ops

The system MUST restore `.memory/manifest.json` churn before any git or
engram operation, and this restore MUST be idempotent and safe to repeat.

#### Scenario: Manifest restored first
- GIVEN a `.memory/manifest.json` with local churn
- WHEN `session:start` runs
- THEN the manifest is restored before engram hydration or branch resolution begins

#### Scenario: Idempotent on repeat runs
- GIVEN `session:start` already ran once in the session
- WHEN it runs again
- THEN the manifest restore produces no errors and no unintended state change

### Requirement: REQ-4 Local Engram Hydration

The system MUST hydrate local engram from `.memory/` using
`memory/cli.mjs import` (a local-only operation).

#### Scenario: Engram hydrated from local store
- GIVEN `.memory/` contains exported chunks
- WHEN `session:start` runs
- THEN local engram is populated via `import` with no network call

### Requirement: REQ-5 Branch-to-Change Resolution

The system MUST derive the active change(s) from the current git branch by
extracting an `issue-<N>` token and matching it against
`openspec/changes/*` directory names.

#### Scenario: Exactly one match
- GIVEN the current branch contains `issue-138`
- AND `openspec/changes/issue-138-session-start` exists
- WHEN resolution runs
- THEN that single change is returned and shown in the context block

#### Scenario: Zero matches
- GIVEN the current branch is `main` or has no `issue-<N>` token, or no matching dir exists
- WHEN resolution runs
- THEN no change context is returned, no error is thrown, and the output shows a graceful "no active change" line

#### Scenario: Multiple matches
- GIVEN the branch token matches more than one `openspec/changes/*` directory
- WHEN resolution runs
- THEN all matching changes are listed in the output and none is silently dropped

### Requirement: REQ-6 Active-Ticket Operational Memory

The system MUST reuse the existing `tryFeatureResume()`
(`brain/scripts/memory/lib/auto-resume.mjs`) to surface `next_action`,
`blockers`, and `current_slice` when a `resume.md` exists for the resolved
change.

#### Scenario: Resume context found
- GIVEN a `resume.md` exists for the active change
- WHEN `session:start` runs
- THEN `next_action`, `blockers`, and `current_slice` are printed in the context block

#### Scenario: No resume context
- GIVEN no `resume.md` exists for the active change (or no change is resolved)
- WHEN `session:start` runs
- THEN `tryFeatureResume()` returns null, no error is thrown, and a graceful "no context" line is printed

### Requirement: REQ-7 Deterministic Context Output

The system MUST print a compact, deterministic context block to stdout
containing: brain version (optional/best-effort), current branch, resolved
change(s) (or "none"), and the ticket resume summary (or "none").

#### Scenario: Full context available
- GIVEN manifest restore, engram hydration, branch resolution, and resume all succeed
- WHEN `session:start` completes
- THEN stdout contains all four sections in a stable, parseable order

#### Scenario: Partial context still prints a valid block
- GIVEN branch resolution finds no change
- WHEN `session:start` completes
- THEN the block still prints with an explicit "no active change" section rather than omitting it

### Requirement: REQ-8 i18n Coverage

All user-facing strings produced by `session:start` MUST be sourced from
`session.*` keys defined in `en.mjs` (canonical) and `es.mjs`.

#### Scenario: Key coverage test passes
- GIVEN the i18n coverage test suite
- WHEN it runs against `session.*` keys
- THEN every key in `en.mjs` has a matching key in `es.mjs` and no string is hardcoded outside the i18n layer

### Requirement: REQ-9 day:start Non-Regression

After extracting `lib/git-branch.mjs` and `lib/memory-manifest.mjs` for reuse
by `session:start`, `day:start` MUST preserve its existing observable
behavior (networked steps, output, exit codes).

#### Scenario: day:start behavior unchanged after extraction
- GIVEN the pre-extraction `day:start` test suite
- WHEN `day:start` is run after the libs are extracted and consumed
- THEN all prior `day:start` behavior and tests still pass unchanged

content:
**sdd/issue-138-session-start/design**

**What**: Technical design for issue-138 `session:start` — a universal, read-only, LOCAL-ONLY session context loader (agent-agnostic core + thin Claude Code SessionStart adapter).

**Why**: `day:start` is heavy/networked/once-daily and does not survive context compaction; resuming agents lose brain's operational context. Need a fast local-only "load my brain context now" entry point.

**Where**: openspec/changes/issue-138-session-start/design.md. New files: brain/scripts/session-start.mjs, brain/scripts/lib/git-branch.mjs, brain/scripts/lib/memory-manifest.mjs. Modified: brain/scripts/day-start.mjs (consumer), brain/scripts/memory/backends/engram.mjs (_getGitBranch wrapper), .claude/settings.json (merge SessionStart hook), i18n/en.mjs+es.mjs (session.* keys), package.json (session:start script).

**Key decisions**:
1. session-start.mjs is import-time pure (side effects guarded by `process.argv[1] === fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)` like brain-start.mjs). 5 ordered step fns: restoreManifestChurn, hydrateEngram(cli.mjs import), resolveBranchChange, loadTicketMemory(EXISTING tryFeatureResume), renderContextBlock. runSessionStart ALWAYS exitCode:0 (best-effort; never block a session).
2. lib/git-branch.mjs `currentBranch(cwd,{_spawn})`: `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`, returns string on named branch, null on detached HEAD ("HEAD" sentinel) AND non-git. De-dups day-start.mjs:150 and engram.mjs:296 (_getGitBranch becomes `currentBranch(root) ?? 'unknown'`). Chose rev-parse over --show-current because engram already relies on the "HEAD" detached behavior.
3. lib/memory-manifest.mjs `restoreManifestChurn(cwd)`: extracts day-start.mjs:117-129; returns {restored:bool}; never throws.
4. deriveChangeFromBranch(branch, changesDir): regex /issue-(\d+)/i → token; readdirSync openspec/changes excluding archive; matches = dir names containing token, sorted. Returns {token, matches[]}. 0/1/N handled by caller (none/resolved/ambiguous-list-all). NEVER throws.
5. No-network: 3 layers — (a) import-graph allowlist (no day-start/vcs/installer/pull path), (b) runtime assertLocalArgv allowlist gate (git status|restore|rev-parse, cli.mjs import, cli.mjs feature-resume) throws on anything else, (c) spy-spawn behavioral test asserting no pull/fetch/merge/clone/ls-remote/push/--export. Reuses existing local `import` verb (cli.mjs:7-10) instead of a new offline flag.
6. Claude adapter: MERGE a SessionStart sibling key into .claude/settings.json (keep existing PreToolUse --no-verify blocker). Hook = `npm run session:start`, zero logic. Loader prints context block to STDOUT (SessionStart stdout is injected into agent context); stderr for diagnostics; exit 0 always.
7. Output: deterministic plain-text block (no timestamps/random/ANSI), fixed section order: header/branch/change/memory/manifest/ticket. renderContextBlock is PURE (resolved strings injected) for snapshot tests.
8. i18n session.* namespace; resolve all keys once in CLI entry, pass map into pure renderer (t() is async, renderer stays sync).

**Testing (strict TDD, node:test, zero deps)**: colocated *.test.mjs per unit (git-branch, memory-manifest, deriveChangeFromBranch, renderContextBlock snapshots, runSessionStart with spy deps); no-network tested structurally (allowlist+import-graph) AND behaviorally (spy spawn over full loop); branch→change via temp openspec/changes fixtures; day-start non-regression via isolated lib tests + assert day-start imports the libs.

**Alternatives rejected**: logic-in-settings.json (violates ADR-0002); reuse day:start as-is (heavy/networked); reuse resolveFeature (branch-blind + throws on ambiguity); --show-current (loses detached signal); new --no-network flag on pull (import verb already local); async renderer; any new dependency (brain has ZERO deps, use readdirSync withFileTypes).

**Learned**: Repo nests under brain/ (scripts at brain/scripts/). `import` verb is local (engram sync --import), `pull` is networked (cli.mjs:7-10) — must never call pull. resolveFeature intentionally ignores branch names (feature-resolution.mjs:19-20), so a NEW branch-aware resolver is required. t() is async so renderer takes pre-resolved strings.

content:
**issue-138 delivery: chained feature-branch-chain, 3 slices**

**What**: Delivery decision for SDD change issue-138-session-start. Review Workload Forecast = High (~750-930 changed lines, code+tests+i18n). User chose **chained PRs with feature-branch-chain** strategy.

**Why**: Exceeds the 400-line budget; session:start is infra distributed to consumers, so no partial session-start should land in main until complete.

**Chain plan (feature-branch-chain)**:
- Tracker branch: `feature/issue-138-session-start` (off main). Only the tracker merges to main.
- PR1 → tracker: shared libs + consumer refactors (lib/git-branch.mjs, lib/memory-manifest.mjs, refactor day-start.mjs + engram.mjs _getGitBranch). Behavior-preserving, ~150-190 lines. (REQ-3, REQ-9)
- PR2 → PR1 branch: session-start core + no-network guarantee (deriveChangeFromBranch, 5 steps, runSessionStart, assertLocalArgv, renderContextBlock, CLI entry, import-graph + spy-spawn no-network tests). ~350-420 lines — re-split if >400 during apply. (REQ-2,5,6,7)
- PR3 → PR2 branch: i18n session.* keys (en+es), package.json session:start script, merged SessionStart hook in .claude/settings.json. ~120-160 lines. (REQ-1,8)

**Gate**: issue #138 still status:needs-review. Issue-first governance gate will reject any PR until the human applies status:approved. Agent NEVER applies status:approved (Tier 3, issue #124). Code can be implemented locally; PRs cannot open without approval.

**Tasks**: obs 311, topic_key sdd/issue-138-session-start/tasks (26 tasks, RED/GREEN). Strict TDD ON (npm test, node:test).
